seoTitle: Insert Video in Confluence: 2026 Embed Tutorial
metaDescription: See how to insert video in Confluence using the 2026 media macro, from pasting YouTube or Loom links to uploading local files and publishing the player live.
intro: Sending teammates to another tab just to watch a screen recording breaks their focus. This walkthrough covers how to embed video directly inside a Confluence page in 2026, whether you're linking a hosted clip or uploading a file straight from your desktop.

steps.heading: Open the media uploader
steps.body: In edit mode, click your insertion point and type the `/media` slash command to bring up the file and link uploader block.

steps.heading: Paste a smart link
steps.body: For hosted content, paste a YouTube, Vimeo, Loom, or Microsoft Stream URL to auto-generate an interactive player card.

steps.heading: Or upload a local file
steps.body: For files on your machine, click Upload and select the MP4 or MOV directly from your system drive.

steps.heading: Adjust the layout
steps.body: Use the bottom layout toolbar to resize the player's viewport width and wrap surrounding text around the frame.

steps.heading: Publish the page
steps.body: Click the Publish button to make the interactive video player live for your workspace.

faq.question: Can I embed videos from YouTube, Vimeo, or Loom in Confluence?
faq.answer: Yes, pasting a link from any of these platforms into the media block auto-generates a playable smart card.

faq.question: Can I upload a video file directly instead of linking one?
faq.answer: Yes, the media block supports dragging local MP4 or MOV files straight onto the canvas.

faq.question: Will embedding video slow down my page?
faq.answer: The 2026 content canvas streams video in the background so it shouldn't slow down the surrounding text from loading.

faq.question: Can I restrict who sees an embedded video?
faq.answer: Yes, space-wide viewing restrictions let you keep proprietary video assets limited to authorized team members.
